title: 8 Killed: A B-52 Bomber Crashed at Edwards Air Force Base
script: The B-52 just crashed! America's most iconic Cold War bomber — a plane that's been flying since 1952, longer than any other bomber in history — went down yesterday at Edwards Air Force Base in California on a routine test mission. All 8 on board were killed. The crew was a mix of active military, government civilians, and 2 Boeing contractors. Edwards AFB is where Chuck Yeager first broke the sound barrier in 1947. Where America's most experimental aircraft get tested. A B-52 crashing there, on a routine mission, should be nearly impossible — and yet. The airfield is closed. Operations are standing down. The investigation will take months. So what actually goes wrong on a 70-year-old bomber's routine test flight?
